1→# Quick Reference 2→ 3→Fast lookup for creating new templates from Figma designs. 4→ 5→## File Creation Checklist 6→ 7→```markdown 8→Given slug: [slug] 9→ 10→- [ ] Create `src/page-[slug].php` 11→- [ ] Create `src/static/scss/pages/[slug].scss` 12→- [ ] Create `src/static/js/pages/[slug].bundle.js` 13→- [ ] Create `src/static/media/images/[slug]/` (for template images) 14→- [ ] Add '[slug]' to HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES in `src/functions.php` 15→- [ ] Run `yarn build` from themes/HoneLandingPages/ 16→- [ ] Test at http://localhost:8081/[wordpress-page-slug]/ 17→``` 18→ 19→## Template Header (Copy/Paste) 20→ 21→```php 22→ '$45 test + online doctor consultation included. Start Now', 36→ 'linkbar_link' => $checkout_url, 37→]; 38→ 39→wp_enqueue_script('hone-page-' . $template_name); 40→ 41→get_header(); 42→?> 43→``` 44→ 45→## Template Footer (Copy/Paste) 46→ 47→```php 48→ 49→``` 50→ 51→With Trustpilot: 52→```php 53→ 54→ 55→``` 56→ 57→## SCSS File Header (Copy/Paste) 58→ 59→```scss 60→/** 61→ * [Display Name] 62→ * Page-specific styles for page-[slug].php 63→ */ 64→@use '../variables/colors' as colors; 65→@use '../variables/fonts' as fonts; 66→@use '../variables/layout' as layout; 67→ 68→.[slug] { 69→ // All template styles scoped to template class 70→} 71→``` 72→ 73→## JS Bundle Header (Copy/Paste) 74→ 75→```javascript 76→/** 77→ * [Display Name] 78→ * Page-specific JavaScript for page-[slug].php 79→ */ 80→import { initCarousel } from '../utilities/carousel.js'; 81→import '../utilities/accordion.js'; 82→// import { initQuiz } from '../utilities/quiz.js'; 83→// import { initStickyCTA } from '../utilities/stickyCTA.js'; 84→ 85→document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() { 86→ // Carousel initialization 87→ // initCarousel({}, '.reviews'); 163→ } 164→} 165→``` 166→ 167→## Figma → SCSS Translation Cheat Sheet 168→ 169→| Figma Tailwind | SCSS Equivalent | 170→|---------------|-----------------| 171→| `bg-[#F1F0EF]` | `background-color: colors.$color-gray-light;` | 172→| `bg-[#0E0B20]` | `background-color: colors.$color-black;` | 173→| `bg-[#F8F93F]` | `background-color: colors.$color-yellow;` | 174→| `text-[54px]` | `font-size: 54px;` | 175→| `font-['STIX_Two_Text']` | `font-family: fonts.$STIX-Two-Text;` | 176→| `font-['DM_Sans']` | `font-family: fonts.$DM-Sans;` | 177→| `font-['Azeret_Mono']` | `font-family: fonts.$Azeret-Mono;` | 178→| `leading-none` | `line-height: 1;` | 179→| `leading-[1.1]` | `line-height: 1.1;` | 180→| `leading-[1.4]` | `line-height: 1.4;` | 181→| `tracking-[-1.08px]` | `letter-spacing: -1.08px;` | 182→| `gap-[24px]` | `gap: 24px;` | 183→| `p-[64px]` | `padding: 64px;` | 184→| `px-[32px] py-[12px]` | `padding: 12px 32px;` | 185→| `rounded-[2px]` | `border-radius: 2px;` | 186→| `rounded-[15px]` | `border-radius: 15px;` | 187→| `flex flex-col` | `display: flex; flex-direction: column;` | 188→| `flex-[1_0_0]` | `flex: 1;` | 189→| `items-center` | `align-items: center;` | 190→| `justify-center` | `justify-content: center;` | 191→| `shrink-0` | `flex-shrink: 0;` | 192→| `overflow-clip` | `overflow: hidden;` | 193→| `self-stretch` | `align-self: stretch;` | 194→ 195→## Carousel HTML Pattern 196→ 197→```html 198→ 199→

'/static/media/images/...'` | 291→| PHP string syntax error | Escape quotes: `'I\'m'` not `'I'm'` | 292→| Carousel not working | Check jQuery is loaded, correct prefix naming, import carousel.js | 293→ 294→## PHP String Escaping 295→ 296→```php 297→// CORRECT 298→'I\'m able to share...' 299→'It\'s been over a year...' 300→'5\'8" and 210 pounds' 301→ 302→// WRONG (will cause syntax error) 303→'I'm able to share...' 304→``` 305→ 306→## Image Path Pattern 307→ 308→```php 309→// Template-specific images 310→$theme_folder . '/static/media/images/' . $template_name . '/hero.webp' 311→ 312→// Shared images (icons, logos) 313→$theme_folder . '/static/media/images/trustpilot-stars.svg' 314→$theme_folder . '/static/media/images/icon-shipping.svg' 315→``` 316→ 317→## Tracking Attributes 318→ 319→```php 320→// All clickable elements 321→data-trackable="[section]-[element]" 322→data-interaction="0" 323→ 324→// Examples 325→data-trackable="hero-cta" 326→data-trackable="pricing-cta" 327→data-trackable="how-it-works-cta" 328→data-trackable="quiz-start" 329→``` 330→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Now I have a good picture. Let me analyze the `page-mens-hrt-enhanced-pdp-1.php` template and note what's different/notable compared to what's already documented in the figma-to-code files.

Key observations from this template:

**Class naming scheme:**
- Uses descriptive kebab-case section names as the primary class: `boost-your-testosterone`, `marquee trustpilot-ratings`, `how-it-works social-videos`, `benefits-timeline`, `proven-benefits`, `injections-section`, `testimonials`, `alternatives`
- Uses utility modifier classes on sections: `border-bottom`, `extended-center-border-below`, `extended-center-border-above`


- Sections include anchor IDs for navigation like `id="injections-section"`
- Container structure typically flows from `.container` to `.wrapper` or direct children
- Uses flat BEM-like naming within sections (`.content-wrapper`, `.media-wrapper`, `.title-block`, `.content-block`, `.grid-block`) rather than the double-underscore BEM convention documented in SECTION_LIBRARY.md
- Frequently combines multiple classes for compound effects, like `carousel-wrapper carousel social-videos-carousel` or `wrapper carousel-wrapper carousel testimonials-carousel`

This naming approach differs from the SECTION_LIBRARY.md documentation, which uses a different BEM structure.

The actual pattern I'm seeing is a consistent section structure with a container, wrapper, and block hierarchy, where wrappers group content and blocks define specific roles, all using flat compound class naming rather than parent-child BEM relationships. I'm seeing the rest of the naming conventions now—the carousel pattern uses a prefix-based approach for navigation controls like prev, next, and dots, while the FAQ icons use a two-span structure to create the plus-to-times animation effect.

The template also relies on `add_action` for script enqueuing with data localization, handles page-specific disclaimers through a global variable, and the CTA block function accepts anchor links for smooth scrolling to sections.

Now I'm thinking about what documentation gaps exist in the figma-to-code guides. The current BEM notation in the section library doesn't align with how the actual enhanced PDP templates are structured, so I should create a focused guide that documents the real naming conventions being used across these templates. Let me review what documentation files already exist and identify where to add this clarification.

Looking at the existing documentation, I can see there's a clear mismatch between the BEM-style class naming documented in the section library and the flat compound naming actually used in the templates. I should create a new file specifically for the enhanced PDP patterns that covers the actual class naming scheme, section modifiers, responsive visibility classes, the FAQ icon pattern, the action enqueueing approaches, the global disclaimers variable, the CTA block function signature, and grid classes. This will serve as a more accurate reference than updating the existing section library.

1→# Design System: Figma Tokens → SCSS Variables
     2→
     3→Complete mapping between Figma design tokens and the theme's SCSS variables/conventions.
     4→
     5→## Colors
     6→
     7→### Figma Token → SCSS Variable
     8→
     9→| Figma Token Name | Hex Value | SCSS Variable | Usage |
    10→|-----------------|-----------|---------------|-------|
    11→| Blue Gray Night/100% | `#0E0B20` | `$color-black` | Primary text, dark backgrounds |
    12→| White | `#FCFCFA` | `$color-white-off` | Card backgrounds, light text on dark |
    13→| White (pure) | `#FFFFFF` | `$color-white` | Pure white backgrounds |
    14→| Gray Light | `#F1F0EF` | `$color-gray-light` | Light section backgrounds, hero bg |
    15→| Gray Mid/100% | `#D2CEC4` | `$color-gray-medium` | Quiz bg, FAQ bg, muted elements |
    16→| Gray Dark/100% | `#605B52` | `$color-gray` | Secondary text, metadata |
    17→| Gray Dark/80% | `rgba(96,91,82,0.8)` | `$color-black-lightest` | Tertiary text, borders |
    18→| Vital Yellow/100% | `#F8F93F` | `$color-yellow` | Primary CTA buttons |
    19→| Vital Yellow/Hover | `#EDEE39` | `$color-yellow-vital-hover` | Stat highlight backgrounds |
    20→| Green - In Range | `#119555` | `$color-green-dark` | Progress bar active segments |
    21→
    22→### Direct Hex Usage (when no variable exists)
    23→
    24→Some colors appear in Figma that are used directly:
    25→- `#D4CDC2` — Hero image placeholder background (use inline)
    26→- `#20C475` — Verified badge green (use inline)
    27→- `rgba(96,91,82,0.8)` — FAQ borders, tertiary text (use `$color-black-lightest`)
    28→
    29→## Typography
    30→
    31→### Font Families
    32→
    33→| Figma Font | SCSS Variable | CSS Value | Usage |
    34→|-----------|---------------|-----------|-------|
    35→| STIX Two Text | `$STIX-Two-Text` | `"STIX Two Text", serif` | Headlines, display text, prices |
    36→| STIX Two Math | `$STIX-Two-Text` | `"STIX Two Text", serif` | Same font family — Figma calls it "Math" variant |
    37→| DM Sans | `$DM-Sans` | `"DM Sans", sans-serif` | Body text, buttons, labels, subheadings |
    38→| Azeret Mono | `$Azeret-Mono` | `"Azeret Mono", monospace` | Stats/numbers, labels, prices |
    39→
    40→**Important:** Figma references "STIX Two Math" — use the same `$STIX-Two-Text` variable. They're the same font family.
    41→
    42→### Typography Scale
    43→
    44→| Figma Token | Font | Size | Weight | Line-Height | Letter-Spacing | SCSS Approach |
    45→|------------|------|------|--------|-------------|----------------|---------------|
    46→| Display/XL | STIX Two Text | 54px | 400 | 1 (54px) | -1.08px (-2%) | Desktop headlines |
    47→| Display/L | STIX Two Text | 36px | 400 | 1 (36px) | -0.72px (-2%) | Tablet/mobile headlines |
    48→| Display/D3 | STIX Two Text | 40px | 400 | 48px | 0 | Quiz questions, section titles |
    49→| Display/D5 | STIX Two Text | 24px | 400 | 32px | 0 | List item prices, section subtitles |
    50→| Display/S | STIX Two Text | 20px | 400 | 0.96 | -1% | Small display text |
    51→| Heading/XL | DM Sans | 28px | 400 | 1.1 | -0.56px (-2%) | Subheadings |
    52→| Heading/L | DM Sans | 22px | 500 | 1.1 | -0.44px (-2%) | Card headings, stat descriptions |
    53→| Heading/M | DM Sans | 18px | 500 | 1.1 | -0.36px (-2%) | Tablet/mobile subheadings |
    54→| Text/T1 | DM Sans | 24px | 400 | 32px | 0 | Listicle item titles |
    55→| Text/T3 | DM Sans | 16px | 400 | 24px | 0 | Regular text |
    56→| Body/L | DM Sans | 16px | 400 | 1.4 | -0.16px (-1%) | Body paragraphs |
    57→| Button/B1 | DM Sans | 16px | 500 | 24px | 0.8px (5%) | CTA buttons |
    58→| Label/L | Azeret Mono | 16px | 400 | 1.2 | 0 | Desktop labels |
    59→| Label/S | Azeret Mono | 12px | 400 | 1.2 | 0 | Mobile labels |
    60→| Numbers/S | Azeret Mono | 18px | 400 | 1.2 | 0 | Small stat numbers |
    61→| Numbers/XS-Strikethrough | Azeret Mono | 14px | 400 | 1.2 | 0 | Crossed-out prices |
    62→
    63→### SCSS Typography Pattern
    64→
    65→```scss
    66→// Display headline (STIX Two Text)
    67→.section-title {
    68→    font-family: $STIX-Two-Text;
    69→    font-weight: 400;
    70→    font-size: 54px;
    71→    line-height: 1;
    72→    letter-spacing: -1.08px;

    79→}
    80→
    81→// Body text (DM Sans)
    82→.body-text {
    83→    font-family: $DM-Sans;
    84→    font-weight: 400;
    85→    font-size: 16px;
    86→    line-height: 1.4;
    87→    letter-spacing: -0.16px;
    88→    color: $color-black;
    89→}
    90→
    91→// Stat number (Azeret Mono)
    92→.stat-number {
    93→    font-family: $Azeret-Mono;
    94→    font-weight: 400;
    95→    font-size: 60px;
    96→    line-height: 1.2;
    97→    color: $color-black;
    98→}
    99→
   100→// Button text (DM Sans Medium)
   101→.cta-button {
   102→    font-family: $DM-Sans;
   103→    font-weight: 500;
   104→    font-size: 16px;
   105→    line-height: 24px;
   106→    letter-spacing: 0.8px;
   107→}
   108→```
   109→
   110→## Spacing System
   111→
   112→### Section Padding (from Figma)
   113→
   114→| Context | Desktop | Tablet | Mobile |
   115→|---------|---------|--------|--------|
   116→| Section horizontal padding | 64px | 32px | 15px |
   117→| Section vertical padding | 64-80px | 40-56px | 22.5-40px |
   118→| Card internal padding | 16px | 16px | 16px |
   119→| Content gap (large) | 40-80px | 32-40px | 15-24px |
   120→| Content gap (medium) | 24-32px | 16-24px | 15-16px |
   121→| Content gap (small) | 8-16px | 8-16px | 8px |
   122→| Card gap | 24px | 24px | 16px |
   123→
   124→### SCSS Spacing Pattern
   125→
   126→```scss
   127→.section {
   128→    padding: 80px 64px;
   129→
   130→    @media #{$medium-screen-and-smaller} {
   131→        padding: 56px 32px;
   132→    }
   133→
   134→    @media #{$small-screen-and-smaller} {

   138→```
   139→
   140→## Shadows
   141→
   142→### Soft Shadow (Cards)

   144→// Figma: "Soft Shadow"
   145→box-shadow:
   146→    0px 4px 10px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.09),
   147→    0px 18px 18px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.07),
   148→    0px 40px 24px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04),

   152→
   153→### Quiz Answer Shadow
   154→```scss
   155→// Figma: Quiz answer buttons
   156→box-shadow:
   157→    0px 3px 6px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1),
   158→    0px 10px 10px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.09),
   159→    0px 23px 14px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05),
   160→    0px 40px 16px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.01),
   161→    0px 63px 18px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0);
   162→```
   163→
   164→## Border Radius
   165→
   166→| Element | Radius |
   167→|---------|--------|
   168→| CTA buttons | 2px |
   169→| Stat/Review cards | 15px |
   170→| Step number circles | 1000px (full circle) |
   171→| Arrow navigation buttons | 40px (pill shape) |
   172→| Card images | 8px |
   173→
   174→## Component Patterns
   175→
   176→### CTA Button
   177→```scss
   178→.cta-button {
   179→    background-color: $color-yellow;
   180→    color: $color-black;
   181→    font-family: $DM-Sans;
   182→    font-weight: 500;
   183→    font-size: 16px;
   184→    line-height: 24px;
   185→    letter-spacing: 0.8px;
   186→    padding: 12px 32px;
   187→    border-radius: 2px;
   188→    border: none;
   189→    cursor: pointer;

   194→

   200→
   201→### Dark CTA Button (Quiz Selected Answer)
   202→```scss

   204→    background-color: $color-black;
   205→    color: $color-white;

   207→}

   209→
   210→### Trustpilot Badge Row
   211→```
   212→Structure: "Excellent" text | Star rating SVG | "X,XXX reviews on" text | Trustpilot logo
   213→Font: DM Sans, 12px, regular
   214→All elements inline-flex, vertically centered, 8px gap
   215→```
   216→
   217→## Image Conventions
   218→
   219→### Hero Images
   220→- Desktop: Side-by-side with text (50/50 split, 720px each at 1440px viewport)
   221→- Tablet: Stacked — image on top, full width (768px)
   222→- Mobile: Stacked — image on top, full width (393px)
   223→- Use `object-fit: cover` for responsive sizing
   224→- Format: `.webp` preferred
   225→
   226→### Review/Card Images
   227→- Aspect ratio: 16:9 (352×198 at card width)
   228→- `object-fit: cover`
   229→- Border-radius: 0 (flush with card edges)
   230→
   231→### Step/How-It-Works Images
   232→- Border-radius: 8px
   233→- Numbered badge overlay: 56px circle, yellow background, positioned top-left 16px inset
   234→
   235→### Listicle Images
   236→- Fixed height: 416px
   237→- Width: ~48% of viewport (688px at 1440px)
   238→- `object-fit: cover`
   239→- Alternating left/right layout (odd items: text left, image right)

Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me document my observations about the `page-mens-hrt-enhanced-pdp-1.php` template in a new file in `.local/figma-to-code/`.

Key observations:
1. **Class naming scheme** - Uses flat compound naming (NOT BEM). The SECTION_LIBRARY.md documents BEM-style (`hero__title`) but actual templates use flat names (`title-block`, `content-wrapper`).
2. **Section modifier utility classes** - `border-bottom`, `extended-center-border-below`, `extended-center-border-above`
3. **Container/wrapper/block vocabulary** - `container`, `*-wrapper`, `*-block`, `*-item`, `*-card`


4. **Grid and layout patterns** - Uses `grid` and `grid-2-col` classes alongside responsive visibility utilities like `desktop-only` and `tablet-and-smaller-only`
5. **Interactive elements** - FAQ sections use a two-span pattern for toggle animations, and scripts are enqueued with `wp_localize_script` to pass data to the frontend
6. **Page-specific customization** - Supports `$additional_disclaimers` for footer content and a flexible `render_cta_block()` function that handles secondary button styling
7. **Major page sections** - The template includes a hero with video, a scrolling testimonials marquee, video carousel, horizontal timeline, two-column benefits grid, gallery with FAQ, testimonials carousel, and product alternatives grid

Now I'm documenting these patterns in a reference file.

input.content:
# Enhanced PDP Naming Conventions Observed patterns from `page-mens-hrt-enhanced-pdp-1.php`. Use this as the authoritative reference for class naming — it reflects what's actually in production templates. > **Note:** `SECTION_LIBRARY.md` uses BEM-style naming (`hero__title`, `hero__content`) as an abstraction layer. Actual templates use **flat compound naming** instead. Follow the flat style documented here. --- ## Section Class Naming Sections use a **primary descriptive name** plus optional **modifier utility classes**: ```php
``` ### Examples from this template | Primary Name | Modifier Classes | Notes | |---|---|---| | `boost-your-testosterone hero` | `border-bottom` | Two semantic names on one section | | `marquee trustpilot-ratings` | `border-bottom extended-center-border-below` | | | `how-it-works social-videos` | `extended-center-border-below` | | | `benefits-timeline` | `border-bottom extended-center-border-above` | | | `proven-benefits` | `border-bottom extended-center-border-above` | | | `injections-section` | `border-bottom extended-center-border-below` | Has `id="injections-section"` for anchor | | `testimonials` | `extended-center-border-below` | | | `alternatives` | `extended-center-border-above` | | ### Section Border Modifier Classes These control the decorative center-line border between sections: | Class | Effect | |---|---| | `border-bottom` | Adds a bottom border to the section | | `extended-center-border-below` | Extends a vertical center border downward into the next section | | `extended-center-border-above` | Extends a vertical center border upward into the previous section | These can be combined. Typical pairings: - `border-bottom extended-center-border-below` — section ends with border that continues down - `border-bottom extended-center-border-above` — section receives border from above --- ## Container/Wrapper/Block Vocabulary Consistent suffix vocabulary used throughout all templates: | Suffix | Role | Example | |---|---|---| | `.container` | Outermost layout container inside `
` | `
` | | `.container.grid` | Grid layout variant | `
` | | `.container.grid.grid-2-col` | 2-column grid | `
` | | `*-wrapper` | Grouping container for a region | `.media-wrapper`, `.content-wrapper`, `.title-wrapper`, `.carousel-wrapper`, `.badges-wrapper` | | `*-block` | A self-contained content unit | `.title-block`, `.content-block`, `.media-block`, `.list-block`, `.cta-block`, `.grid-block`, `.faq-block` | | `*-item` or `*-card` | Repeating unit within a loop | `.review-item`, `.testimonial-item`, `.faq-item`, `.timeline-card`, `.benefits-item`, `.grid-item` | ### Typical Section Structure ```php
``` --- ## Responsive Visibility Classes ```php class="desktop-only" // Hidden on tablet and smaller class="tablet-and-smaller-only" // Hidden on desktop ``` Used in `injections-section` to swap between a static desktop grid and a mobile carousel for the same image set. --- ## Grid Classes on `.container` ```php
// Basic grid
// Two-column grid ``` Column children can have `.border-right` to add a divider: ```php
``` --- ## Carousel Pattern (Actual) The `.container` child gets both a generic `.carousel` class and a **prefix-specific** class. The JS utility picks it up by prefix. ```php
...
``` Navigation arrows and dots: ```php
``` ```javascript initCarousel({}, '.[prefix]'); ``` --- ## FAQ Icon Pattern (Actual) The actual template uses **two `` elements** for the +/× animation, NOT a plain `+` text character: ```php ``` The `.line-vertical` is rotated/hidden via CSS on expand. --- ## `render_cta_block()` Signature ```php render_cta_block($url, $label, $secondary = false, $extra_class = ''); // Scroll to anchor on same page: render_cta_block('#injections-section', 'Test my T-Levels'); // Checkout URL, default primary style: render_cta_block($checkout_url, 'Test My T-Levels'); // With custom class on the wrapper: render_cta_block($checkout_url, 'Test My T-Levels', false, 'proven-benefits-cta-block'); ``` --- ## Script Enqueuing with JS Data When a template needs to pass PHP data to JavaScript (e.g., `themeFolder`), use `add_action` + `wp_localize_script` instead of a direct enqueue call: ```php add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', function() use ($template_name, $theme_folder) { wp_enqueue_script('hone-page-' . $template_name); wp_localize_script('hone-page-' . $template_name, 'honePageData', array( 'themeFolder' => $theme_folder )); }); ``` In JS, access via `window.honePageData.themeFolder`. --- ## `$additional_disclaimers` Global For page-specific legal disclaimers appended in the footer: ```php global $class_names, $additional_disclaimers; $additional_disclaimers = [ 'Results may vary. Medications are prescribed if deemed to be clinically necessary...' ]; ``` --- ## Sections in This Template (Reference) Ordered as they appear on the page: 1. **`boost-your-testosterone hero`** — Video autoplay + content split with bullet list, badges 2. **`marquee trustpilot-ratings`** — Trustpilot static badge + CSS marquee of short review quotes 3. **`how-it-works social-videos`** — Carousel of short-form review videos with play/mute controls 4. **`benefits-timeline`** — Horizontal scroll carousel of day-milestone cards (Day 7/30/60/90) 5. **`proven-benefits`** — 2-col grid: image left, 3×2 icon benefits grid right + CTA 6. **`injections-section`** — Product gallery (desktop grid / mobile thumbs carousel) + content + inline FAQ 7. **`testimonials`** — Testimonial carousel with star ratings, nav arrows + dots 8. **`alternatives`** — Product alternatives grid (oral troches, cream) + CTA

content.text:
Created `ENHANCED_PDP_NAMING.md`. Here's a summary of what I captured:

**Key findings from this template:**

1. **Flat class naming, not BEM** — `SECTION_LIBRARY.md` uses `hero__title` style, but actual templates use flat names like `.title-block`, `.content-wrapper`. The new file flags this discrepancy.

2. **Section modifier utility classes** — `border-bottom`, `extended-center-border-below`, `extended-center-border-above` control the decorative vertical center-line between sections. They're commonly combined.

3. **`*-wrapper` / `*-block` / `*-item` vocabulary** — consistent suffixes with specific roles (wrapper = grouping container, block = self-contained content unit, item/card = loop repeater).

4. **Responsive visibility** — `desktop-only` / `tablet-and-smaller-only` used to swap between layout variants of the same content.

5. **FAQ icon** — uses two `<span class="icon-line">` children (not a plain `+`), controlled by CSS transforms.

6. **`render_cta_block()` full signature** — 4th param is an extra class on the wrapper; 1st param can be an anchor `#id` for in-page scroll.

7. **Script enqueuing with `wp_localize_script`** — pattern for passing PHP data to JS via `window.honePageData`.

8. **`$additional_disclaimers` global** — for page-specific legal copy in the footer.
